item.title: AstraZeneca's Imfinzi/Imjudo (EMERALD Trial)
item.description:
• Summary: AstraZeneca reports positive results from the EMERALD trial, showing that the combination of Imfinzi (durvalumab) and Imjudo (tremelimumab) significantly delays liver cancer progression. This win strengthens AstraZeneca's position in immuno-oncology, with the dual checkpoint inhibitor regimen demonstrating efficacy in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC).
  • MoAs: Imfinzi (durvalumab): PD-L1 inhibitor; Imjudo (tremelimumab): CTLA-4 inhibitor
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News, ApexOnco

item.title: ORIC Pharmaceuticals (Prostate Cancer Drug)
item.description:
• Summary: ORIC Pharmaceuticals announces plans to advance a prostate cancer drug to Phase 3, but investor doubts about the combination strategy lead to a stock decline. The company faces a reality check as it chases competitors like Pfizer, with concerns over clinical design and market differentiation.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: ApexOnco, Fierce Biotech, Endpoints News

item.title: Zai Lab & Amgn (DLL3 Collaboration)
item.description:
• Summary: Zai Lab and Amgen have teamed up to develop DLL3-targeted therapies, with Zai Lab exploring the potential of combining two DLL3-targeting approaches. This collaboration includes a milestone payment to Janux Therapeutics.
  • MoAs: DLL3-targeted therapies, such as bispecific T-cell engagers or antibody-drug conjugates, work by binding to DLL3 on cancer cells and engaging immune cells or delivering cytotoxic payloads.
  • Sources: Oncology Pipeline, Endpoints News

item.title: IO Biotech (Cancer Vaccine Failure)
item.description:
• Summary: IO Biotech will file for bankruptcy after the failure of its cancer vaccine in a key trial. The vaccine, which targeted specific antigens, did not meet efficacy endpoints, leading to regulatory roadblocks and shutdown.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: FDA Oncology Approvals (March 2026)
item.description:
• Summary: In March 2026, the FDA issued several decisions on oncology therapies, including approvals and rejections. Key developments likely involve new indications for established drugs or novel agents, impacting treatment paradigms in cancer care.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: ApexOnco

item.title: Orca Bio (Cell Therapy Delay)
item.description:
• Summary: The FDA has delayed its decision on Orca Bio's cell therapy, possibly Orca-T, after the company submitted additional manufacturing data. This setback could affect the timeline for this promising oncology treatment.
  • MoAs: Orca Bio's cell therapy is an allogeneic T-cell immunotherapy designed to target cancer cells while minimizing graft-versus-host disease.
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: Radiopharmaceuticals/Therapeutics
item.description:
• Summary: Radiopharmaceuticals are gaining momentum in cancer treatment, with isotopes like lutetium-177 and radium-223 offering targeted radiotherapy options. This "radiotherapeutics moment" is changing the landscape of oncology care.
  • MoAs: Lutetium Lu 177 dotatate targets somatostatin receptors on neuroendocrine tumors, delivering radiation directly to cancer cells. Radium-223 dichloride mimics calcium and targets bone metastases, emitting alpha particles to kill cancer cells.
  • Sources: Biospace, Biospace
